title: Seasonal Cut Fruit Bowl
description: A simple mixed fruit bowl built from what is in season, brightened with lemon, chaat masala, and mint. First-class fruit course for any main meal slot.
explanation: Fruit should be a deliberate plan item, not an afterthought. This one recipe covers breakfast, lunch finish, dinner side, or evening snack by changing the portion and the seasonal mix.
Default bowl is nut-free, soy-free, gluten-free, Jain, and no-onion/no-garlic. Optional nuts or seeds stay in variations only.
ingredients: 3 cups Mixed Seasonal Fruit, peeled and cut (see seasonal guide below)
Pinch of Black Salt or regular salt (optional)
instructions: Wash, peel as needed, and cut fruit into bite-size pieces. Aim for 2–3 different fruits for color and texture.
Place fruit in a bowl. Add lemon juice and toss gently.
Sprinkle chaat masala, roasted cumin, and a pinch of salt if using. Toss again.
Top with torn mint. Serve immediately for best texture.
stepNotes: Cut fruit just before eating when possible; apples and bananas brown if they sit.
Keep the bowl mostly fruit; spices are accents, not a coating.
For lunch or dinner as a side, use a smaller portion than for breakfast.
If packing ahead, keep lemon and spices separate and toss at serving time.

variationSections.title: Seasonal fruit maps
items: Summer: mango, watermelon, muskmelon, chickoo, banana.
Monsoon: apple, pear, pomegranate, banana, papaya (ripe).
Winter: orange, guava, papaya, amla (sliced thin), sweet lime, pomegranate.
Year-round anchors: banana, apple, papaya when available.

variationSections.title: Optional add-ins (not default)
items: A spoon of pumpkin or sunflower seeds for crunch (still nut-free).
A few almonds or walnuts only if nut-free is not required.
A dollop of soy curd turns this toward a breakfast bowl—see Soy Curd Fruit Bowl for the soy path.
